summary: Elder Bednar teaches that enduring to the end is not mere perseverance but a lifelong Christlike transformation through grace, covenants, spiritual gifts, and especially charity—the pure love of Christ.
haiku:
Grace changes the heart
Charity endures all things
Christ makes us like Him
key_points: Enduring to the end means becoming a new creature in Christ, not simply surviving hardship.
Spiritual gifts, especially charity, are divine blessings that help us become more like the Savior.
Charity is both something we do and something that can possess and transform us through the grace of Jesus Christ.
scriptures_and_gospel_principles: Doctrine and Covenants 121:29 — blessings promised to those who endure valiantly for the gospel
3 Nephi 15:9 — look unto Christ and endure to the end
Doctrine and Covenants 14:7 — eternal life is the greatest gift of God
Mosiah 3:19 — putting off the natural man through the Atonement of Christ
Moroni 7:45–48 — charity is the pure love of Christ and endureth forever
Articles of Faith 1:13 — doing good, seeking virtue, and enduring all things
Doctrine and Covenants 93:12–20 — receiving grace for grace and becoming like Christ
The doctrine of spiritual rebirth and the mighty change of heart
The principle that grace enables gradual Christlike transformation
questions_to_ponder: How does this talk change my understanding of the phrase “endure to the end”?
In what ways do I sometimes think of enduring as merely surviving rather than becoming more like Christ?
How can I more earnestly seek the spiritual gift of charity?
What would change in my relationships if charity began to more fully possess me?
How have covenants, ordinances, and the gift of the Holy Ghost helped transform my desires and character?
Where do I need to rely more fully on the Savior’s grace instead of only my own effort?
